summary: Iteration 2 (QR + Scan + new analytics) tested end-to-end. Backend: 38/38 pytest pass (23 prior regression + 15 new). New tests cover POST /members returning qr_payload+qr_image, GET /members/:id/qr (including 404 + lazy regen), POST /attendance/scan with JSON payload (checked_in) -> duplicate within 10 min -> raw member_id -> invalid id 404 -> expired-member blocked (no record). Analytics: peak-hours returns 24 buckets '00'..'23' with int values, absent-members returns list of {id,name,last_visit,days_absent}, both require auth. Frontend (mobile web, 390x844): login works (note: button testID is 'login-submit-button' not 'login-submit-btn'), dashboard qa-scan navigates to /scanner; web fallback shows simulate-scan; clicking it returns 'CHECKED IN' result sheet with scan-member-name='Rahul Sharma' and scan-again-btn works; members list -> detail -> view-qr-btn -> /member/[id]/qr renders member-qr-image + send-whatsapp-btn (clickable, not disabled); /member/new form has input-address + input-emergency, after save redirects to /member/{id}/qr showing QR + WhatsApp button. One missing UI element: ATTENDANCE tab does NOT show a SCAN button — only CHECK-IN (open-checkin-btn) is present. Demo data 'Vikram Singh' (expired) and 'Ananya Rao' present.

ui_bugs.issue: Attendance screen is missing the 'SCAN' button next to 'CHECK-IN'. Only open-checkin-btn renders in the header. Per problem statement, a scan-btn should navigate to /scanner.

action_items: Add SCAN button on /attendance screen with testID='scan-btn' that navigates to /scanner (alongside the existing CHECK-IN button).

Optional: In /app/frontend/app/member/new.tsx the initial form state object does not include 'address' or 'emergency_contact' keys (TextInputs end up uncontrolled until first keystroke). It works functionally but is inconsistent — add address:'' and emergency_contact:'' to initial useState for cleanliness.
critical_code_review_comments: member/new.tsx: useState initial form lacks 'address' and 'emergency_contact' fields though they are read in submit() and bound via setField. Functionally OK because setField creates them, but TextInputs are briefly uncontrolled. Low risk.
scanner.tsx web fallback: simulate-scan picks api.listMembers()[0] without filtering — fine for testing, but in production the first member could be inactive/expired; consider clarifying via UI copy.
qr.tsx: Linking.canOpenURL('whatsapp://...') will reject on web; the catch correctly falls back to wa.me. Verified clickable in headless preview (button not disabled).
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted ephemeral TEST_* members via API tests and UI flow. No residue.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login button testID is 'login-submit-button' (not 'login-submit-btn'). All other testIDs in problem statement are correct: qa-scan, simulate-scan, scan-member-name, scan-again-btn, view-qr-btn, member-qr-image, send-whatsapp-btn, input-address, input-emergency, save-member-btn. Backend test files: test_gymflow_api.py (regression) + test_gymflow_qr.py (iteration 2 features). Demo data includes 6 members; 'Rahul Sharma' is the first member returned by listMembers (used by simulate-scan).
rca_of_issue: Attendance SCAN button missing: app/(tabs)/attendance.tsx never adds a TouchableOpacity with testID='scan-btn'. Header only renders the CHECK-IN button. Fix: insert a sibling button that calls router.push('/scanner').
